provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Arms and Ammunition Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Commissioner of Police and licensing officers
Arms and Ammunition Board
provisions.content:
4. Arms and Ammunition Board
(1) The Arms and Ammunition Quota Board established under section 4 of the repealed Act, is hereby continued under the new name Arms and Ammunition Board.
[ 13 of 2023, s 5. ]
(2) The Board shall consist of such members as the Minister may, by Notice published in the Gazette , appoint.
[ 13 of 2023, s 5. ]
(3) A person shall not be appointed as a member of the Board if he or she is a Commissioner or Deputy Commissioner.
[ 13 of 2023, s 5. ]
(4) A member of the Board shall hold office for such period as may be specified in the notice appointing him or her and on the expiration of such period shall be eligible for reappointment for one more term.
[ 13 of 2023, s 5. ]

definitions.definition: any catridge or percussion cap designed for use in the discharge of arms, and includes gunpowder and grenades, bombs and others like missiles, whether capable of use with those arms or not, and any munition containing or designed or adapted to contain any noxious liquid, gas or other things
a licence issued under section 17
a licence issued under section 9

artillery of all kinds, apparatus for the discharge of all kinds of projectiles, explosive or gas-diffusing, flame-throwers, bombs, grenades, machine-guns and rifled small-bore breach-loading weapons, or such arms as may be prescribed, but does not include sporting rifles, or personal or other weapons or apparatus not intended for warlike purposes
the Arms and Ammunition Board referred to in section 4

a person acting as an intermediary that brings together relevant parties and arranges or facilitates a transaction involving firearms in return for some form of benefit, whether financial or otherwise
the Commissioner of Police
an "officer" as defined in the Customs and Excise Duty Act (Cap. 50:01)
a person who is licensed to trade in firearms and ammunition under section 17
a permit issued under section 13
a person licenced under section 21 to repair, test or proves firearms, but not a manufacturer or dealer
a permit issued under section 12
a licence issued under section 22
an officer appointed under section 3(2)
a registered company which makes or assembles arms and ammunition and holding a manufacturer’s licence issued under section 19

any element or replacement element specifically designed for a firearm and essential to its operation, including a barrel, frame or receiver, slide or cylinder, bolt or breech block, and any device designed or adapted to diminish the sound caused by firing a firearm
the Central Arms Registry referred to in section 8
the Arms and Ammunition Act (Cap. 24:01) repealed under section 43
assigned to it in section 14
the conveyance through Botswana to another country of firearms or ammunition that have been imported
as defined in the Wildlife Conservation and National Parks Act (Cap. 38:01).
